name: Current Affairs in General Science (8): India's Renewable Energy Capacity Milestones (2026)

sourceRefs.label: pib.gov.in/pv-magazine.com - India's 288 GW renewable capacity (3rd globally, 31 March 2026); 150+ GW solar milestone; domestic solar manufacturing growth

researchNotes: Eighth Science current-affairs topic, opening Part A's 8th rotation lap. Used 'as of [specific date]' framing throughout to handle the inherently fast-moving nature of capacity-addition figures, rather than presenting a single number as a permanently fixed fact.

reading.intro: Covers India's renewable energy capacity milestones as of early-to-mid 2026.

qa.question: What is India's total renewable energy capacity as of 31 March 2026, and what is its global ranking?
qa.answer: 288 GW — making India the world's THIRD-LARGEST renewable energy nation.

qa.question: How is India's renewable energy capacity split by source (as of 31 March 2026)?
qa.answer: Solar ~55%, Wind ~20%, Large Hydro ~19%, Biomass ~4%, Small Hydro ~2%.

qa.question: What solar capacity milestone did India cross by 31 March 2026, and how did it grow by end-May 2026?
qa.answer: 150.26 GW by 31 March 2026; grew further to approximately 157.05 GW by end of May 2026.

qa.question: How much solar capacity did India add in FY2026, and what was the year-on-year growth rate?
qa.answer: About 44.6 GW added in FY2026 — an 87.2% year-on-year increase in solar installations.

qa.question: How has India's domestic solar MODULE manufacturing capacity grown, as of 31 March 2026?
qa.answer: From 2.3 GW to 172 GW — a dramatic increase in domestic manufacturing capability, not just installed capacity.

questions.prompt: As of 31 March 2026, India's total renewable energy capacity (288 GW) ranks it as the world's:
Which source contributes the largest share of India's renewable energy capacity (as of 31 March 2026)?
India's cumulative installed solar capacity crossed 150 GW by:
India added approximately how much solar capacity in FY2026?
India's domestic solar module manufacturing capacity grew from 2.3 GW to approximately how much (as of 31 March 2026)?
